# v4 Figures Changelog

Output: `Leakage_Aware_SMO_SVM_Manuscript_v4_figures.docx`
Base: the user's uploaded `…v3_phase2_claim_calibrated.docx` (which kept the user's table
size/centering edits but was the pre-polish version and had Google Docs artifacts).
No result values were changed. All figure data come from committed Phase 1/2 outputs.

## Added figures (generated by `scripts/10_manuscript_figures.py`)
- **Figure 1 — workflow schematic** (Methods): conceptual leaky (global) vs guarded (nested)
  pipeline comparison; highlights where feature-selection leakage occurs. Conceptual (no numbers).
- **Figure 2 — performance forest plot** (Results, by Table 5): AUROC and PR-AUC for leaky vs
  guarded with 95% bootstrap CIs (leaky AUROC 0.7705 [0.711-0.824]; guarded 0.7265 [0.653-0.791];
  leaky PR-AUC 0.4016 [0.333-0.497]; guarded 0.3653 [0.281-0.472]). Read from
  `tables/uncertainty/bootstrap_ci.csv`.
- **Figure 6 — feature-selection frequency histogram** (feature-stability section): counts of
  features selected in 1-5 outer folds (102 / 41 / 28 / 23 / 28); stable core (28) and unstable
  tail (102) annotated. Read from `results/pilot_gse25055/nested_selected_features_by_fold.csv`.

## Figure renumbering (clean reading order)
- New Figure 1 = workflow schematic; new Figure 2 = performance forest plot.
- Existing figures renumbered: permutation-null Figure 1 -> 3; per-seed AUROC/gap Figure 2 -> 4;
  stability Figure 3 -> 5. New Figure 6 = feature-frequency histogram.
- In-text reference updated: permutation paragraph now cites "Figure 3"; new in-text references
  added for Figures 1, 2, and 6.

## Re-applied Phase-2 polish (the upload was the pre-polish version)
- "collapsed toward chance" -> "shifted toward chance" (Abstract and Principal findings).
- Removed the internal-process sentences from the Results opening.
- Calibrated three Table 10 interpretation cells (confidence-bounded / cautious wording).
- Added the "same-platform, same-study-family" qualifier for GSE25065 (Abstract and cohort text).

## Restored content lost during the user's table editing (from the approved version on main)
- The **Abstract body** (the upload's abstract was hidden inside a Google Docs content control and
  effectively empty/stale); restored the approved polished abstract (single copy).
- The **"Uncertainty quantification for the leakage gap"** Results paragraph.
- The **"Seed-level robustness across 30 repeated cross-validation runs"** Results paragraph.

## Cleanup / integrity
- Removed leftover Google Docs structured-document-tag artifacts (`goog_rdk` content controls);
  this also eliminated a duplicated stale (pre-polish) abstract. Added the required `pgMar gutter`
  attribute. The document now passes schema validation and renders cleanly (40 pages, 6 figures).
- The user's table formatting (sizes/centering) is preserved.
- Verified: no result values changed; figure captions 1-6 present; no dramatic language
  (proves/confirms/smoking gun/undeniable/catastrophic/mirage); key numbers intact.
